answer.recommended_action: Retry traversal of laser_driver_power_supply to retrieve drive current and supply-rail voltage — this is the single remaining discriminating measurement. If drive current is depressed relative to baseline, the sub.power config change (ev.change_cause_log.power_mode, onset-aligned) is the confirmed mechanism and should be reviewed and corrected. If drive current is nominal, root cause shifts to hyp.laser_aging (intrinsic emitter degradation) and emitter replacement should be evaluated. Do not act on the config change alone without confirming the driver-side electrical state.

hypotheses.label: Signal-processing or firmware calibration drift reducing effective sensitivity
Laser emitter output power declining due to aging or degradation
TEC / thermal regulation fault elevating laser or detector operating temperature and reducing performance
Receiver/detector sensitivity drift (dark current rise, responsivity loss) degrading SNR at range

evidence.summary: Laser output power measured at 41.8 mW vs 48.0 mW baseline — a sustained 13% decline flagged as source-side power loss. Magnitude is consistent with the observed ~8% range degradation (range scales sub-linearly with power). This is a real emitter-side reduction, not a path-loss or receiver-side artifact.

TEC load check nominal: current 1.98 A is 79% of the 2.5 A limit (not saturating), diode temperature 25.2 °C is within the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum, and losing_setpoint=false. Thermal regulation is functioning correctly — no TEC fault detected.

common_mode_check found 3 degraded channels with an onset spread of only 1.25 days, affirming a single shared upstream cause rather than independent per-channel faults. This rules against hypotheses that require independent failure modes and strongly implicates one upstream node (laser emitter or power supply) as the root cause.

Traversal to laser_driver_power_supply returned error 'node_id' — no drive current or supply rail voltage was retrieved. The intended discrimination between hyp.laser_aging (degraded wall-plug efficiency at a nominal drive current) and hyp.upstream_power (reduced drive current from a supply-side fault) remains entirely unresolved. No measurement is asserted per this result.
log.power_mode: a recent config change to sub.power aligns with the degradation onset → the change is the cause
3 degraded channel(s), onset spread 1.25d → common-mode (one upstream cause, not independent faults)

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

With all six hypotheses at equal prior and no evidence yet, the single most discriminating first measurement is laser output power. A degraded result raises hyp.laser_aging and implicates the transmit side of hyp.thermal_tec and hyp.upstream_power while leaving receive-side and processing hypotheses untouched. A nominal result strongly argues AGAINST hyp.laser_aging and hyp.upstream_power (transmit path), cleanly redirecting focus to the receive/processing half of the differential (hyp.detector_drift, hyp.window_contamination, hyp.calibration_drift). This single binary outcome therefore splits the six-hypothesis space into two non-overlapping clusters rather than confirming one hypothesis in isolation.

ev.laser_power_001 confirms a real 13% emitter-side power reduction, but does NOT explain WHY: it could be intrinsic laser aging (hyp.laser_aging) OR thermally-induced efficiency loss because the TEC is failing to regulate junction temperature (hyp.thermal_tec). These two hypotheses are both at log_odds=+2.00 and currently indistinguishable. tec_load_check is the sharpest wedge: a nominal TEC (low duty cycle, stable set-point, on-target temperatures) argues strongly AGAINST hyp.thermal_tec and consolidates the cause in the laser emitter itself; an overloaded or drifting TEC (high duty cycle, rising cold-side temperature, set-point deviation) supports hyp.thermal_tec as the proximate driver of power loss and demotes hyp.laser_aging from primary to secondary. hyp.upstream_power is partially addressed as a side-effect — anomalous TEC load could reflect a shared power-rail sag. Re-running laser_power_check or temp_correlation_check would merely re-confirm already-supported evidence; tec_load_check is the only available check that can simultaneously raise one leading hypothesis and lower another.

ev.laser_power_001 confirms a 13% emitter-side power loss, but does NOT yet tell us whether the loss is intrinsic to the laser diode (hyp.laser_aging — the diode slope-efficiency or threshold has drifted) or is extrinsic and driven by a shared upstream supply/driver fault (hyp.upstream_power — reduced drive current reaching the laser). A common_mode_check probes correlated degradation across co-powered sub-systems: if other downstream loads on the same rail show concurrent, correlated performance drops the result is strong evidence for upstream_power and argues against laser_aging being the isolated cause; if only the laser channel is affected the result argues the opposite — the fault is local to the emitter, consistent with aging. ev.tec_load_001 already weakens hyp.thermal_tec, so the highest-value next discrimination is laser-aging vs. upstream-power.

args.goal: Retrieve laser drive current and supply rail voltage at the laser driver stage to determine whether the 13% optical output decline (ev.laser_power_001: 41.8 mW vs 48.0 mW) is caused by reduced drive current from an upstream supply fault or by degraded wall-plug efficiency of the laser at a nominal drive current.

trace.rationale: Both hyp.laser_aging and hyp.upstream_power sit at log_odds=3.00 — co-leading with equal support. ev.laser_power_001 confirms a real emitter-side optical power loss; ev.common_mode_001 confirms a single shared upstream node as root cause. Neither result tells us WHERE in the upstream chain the fault originates. The decisive discriminator is laser drive current: (a) if drive current is nominal (~baseline mA) but optical output is 13% down, wall-plug efficiency has degraded → laser aging confirmed, upstream_power weakened; (b) if drive current is proportionally reduced, the supply/driver is under-delivering before the laser → upstream_power confirmed, laser_aging weakened. No executed check has yet retrieved drive current or supply voltage; this traversal fills that gap with a single measurement. hyp.calibration_drift is deprioritised because ev.laser_power_001 is a direct physical measurement independent of range-processing firmware. hyp.thermal_tec is near-ruled by ev.tec_load_001 (nominal).
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential
